stories.title: Italy in crisis: federation sounds out Pep Guardiola and Carlo Ancelotti for coach
stories.summary: MILAN: The Italian soccer federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup. Former Italy and AC Milan defender Paolo Maldini was named as the federation's new technical director at the start of the month and was present at a meeting with the Serie A clubs on Thursday, along with new FIGC president Giovanni Malagò and special advisor Leonardo. “Honestly, today we can't give news about what's happening.

stories.title: Ukraine beheads its military as its performance begins to soar
stories.summary: Ukraine replaced its top military and political defence personnel in the past week even as its armed forces arguably achieved their best performance in three years. Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov’s ouster on July 15 was followed six days later by the replacement of Commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskii. Both men were followed out the door by some of their most capable lieutenants. Ukraine seemed to have found a winning formula, making 2026 Russia’s worst year of the war for manpower losses, territory and economic performance at home.

stories.title: YG And The Game Deliver Epic West Coast Verzuz Battle
stories.summary: YG And The Game Deliver Epic West Coast Verzuz Battle Two of Compton’s biggest hip-hop stars joined forces for a historic night celebrating West Coast rap. YG and The Game headlined an iconic Verzuz battle , bringing their biggest records, career highlights, and hometown pride to fans around the world. The highly anticipated event streamed on Apple Music and YouTube through Complex and Verzuz , allowing viewers to experience the showdown from multiple platforms. The special night gave fans a chance to revisit some of the most influential songs from two artists who have helped shape modern West Coast hip-hop.

stories.title: Love Island SPOILER: 'Gameplay' accusations rock the villa as Maya Jama puts Islanders in the hot seat
stories.summary: By on 23rd July 2026 in By on 23rd July 2026 in The events of The Grafties have boiled over into another episode of Love Island , as accusations of 'gameplay' have unsettled the Islanders. In a first look at Thursday's episode, Priya questions Julia over her thoughts on her connection with Aidan. She asks: 'Do you think I’m genuinely with Aidan as a gameplay, to get to the final? ' Julia admits: 'I do think you’ve developed a connection. He would pick you over any new bombshell as that would guarantee the final. ' Priya and Yasmin, Love Island. Pic: ITV The conversation shifts to the wider dynamics in the Villa.

stories.title: Andy Burnham cuts HMRC income tax bills to £0 for people born in these years
stories.summary: State pensioners with no other income will be exempt from tax under new Labour Party government Prime Minister Andy Burnham. Mr Burnham and new Chancellor John Healey have committed to making sure pensioners whose only income is the state pension will not pay income tax. After Mr Burnham committed to keeping the Triple Lock, from next year, the state pension from the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) will be dragged above the personal tax-free allowance of £12,547. 60 a year.

stories.title: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over past jibe
stories.summary: Miquita Oliver apologises to Fearne Cotton over 'awful' comment in the past Updated / Wednesday, 22 Jul 2026 18:15 Getty (L-R) Miquita Oliver and Fearne Cotton - Oliver was let go from her radio job in 2005 following an interview with NME where she was asked how she would improve Top of the Pops and replied that the programme should get rid of Cotton Presenter Miquita Oliver has apologised to Fearne Cotton after Oliver was sacked from BBC Radio 1 in 2005 for making an "awful" comment about her fellow broadcaster.

stories.title: Saudi prince found dead in £600-a-night London hotel room as cause of death unearthed
stories.summary: A Saudi prince who was found dead in a lavish Kensington hotel room was almost three times over the drink-drive limit and had "fatal levels" of the party drug GHP in his system, an inquest heard. Abdullah bin Fahad bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz bin Jalawi al Saud was found dead in his £600-a-night Marriott hotel room in Kensington on the 25 November last year. A cleaner found the prince, 29, lying in the bathroom of his locked fifth-floor room fully clothed. Hotel security and ambulance were called but were unable to resuscitate him . He was sadly pronounced dead at the scene.
stories.trend: saudi prince died in london hotel

stories.title: Why does Zapatero reappear now?
stories.summary: Why does Zapatero reappear now? This Thursday is an anniversary day. It has been exactly 26 years since José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero was elected Secretary General of the PSOE. At that time, he could not have imagined that a quarter of a century later he would be sitting on the set of Mañaneros on TVE giving explanations for two serious criminal cases: one for alleged influence peddling for the Plus Ultra bailout and another for alleged smuggling of millionaire jewelry found in the safe of his office. Why has it appeared now? In fact, the explanations, according to what he himself had announced, should have arrived earlier.

stories.title: Azad Engineering Handovers India's First Indigenous 350 kg Expendable Turbojet Engine to DRDO GTRE
stories.summary: The first indigenous Expendable Turbo Jet Engine of 350 kg thrust class, designed by DRDO’s Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE), has been successfully developed. GTRE had identified Azad Engineering, Hyderabad as Industry partner for the manufacturing and assembly of the engine. On July 22, 2026, the engine was successfully realised and delivered to GTRE by Azad Engineering, a landmark achievement for India’s aerospace and defence ecosystem. The delivery marks the culmination of years of precision engineering, advanced manufacturing, and close partnership between India’s scientific & industrial communities.

stories.title: Man dies of injuries after attack at Regensburg bank: police
stories.summary: Police said on Thursday that a man had died after being critically wounded in an apparent hostage situation at a bank branch in Regensburg, Bavaria. The man was taken to a local hospital but was pronounced dead soon after. A major police operation, including special units and a helicopter, had gathered near the scene on Thursday afternoon. What else do we know about the standoff? Police said they were investigating "a homicide as well as suspected attempted robbery," in an update later on Thursday. "Two people were still inside the building. Special units were able to bring them out of the building in the mean time," police said. At around 6 p. m.

stories.title: Live updates: US strikes Iran for 13th consecutive night
stories.summary: Live Updates Video Ad Feedback Trump adds new requirement to Saudi nuclear deal amid criticism 1:42 • Source: CNN Trump adds new requirement to Saudi nuclear deal amid criticism 1:42 Ad Feedback • No respite from war: The US said it completed a 13th consecutive night of strikes , hours after President Donald Trump signaled he’s not yet ready to negotiate a new ceasefire. Four people were killed , according to state media. Following the latest onslaught, Iran claimed it targeted US assets in Bahrain and Jordan. • Iran rejects ceasefire offer: Tehran rejected a US ceasefire deal which was presented by the Iraqi prime minister, The New York Times reported.

stories.title: TV tonight: documentary reveals the shocking truth behind model family blogger Ruby Franke
stories.summary: The Devil in the Family: The Fall of Ruby Franke 9pm, BBC Two At its peak, Ruby Franke’s parenting YouTube channel was earning £100,000 a month. But, as this documentary series shows, her ostensibly model family was a house of cards. As her ex-husband puts it: “The devil knocked and we opened the door. ” Their downfall culminated in Franke’s arrest for child abuse in 2023 after her emaciated son escaped and summoned help. Thanks to the plentiful footage recorded by Franke herself, it’s easy to sense the roots of this horror. Phil Harrison The Chase Around the World 9pm, ITV1 The shrewd expansion of the Chase universe into a travel contest with a general knowledge quiz attached continues.

stories.title: Denise Julia Releases Her Third EP 'LOVE AGAIN' via EMPIRE
stories.summary: Denise Julia releases her third EP, LOVE AGAIN , via the US-based record label EMPIRE. Following the release of her singles “2GOOD4U,” “CHANGES,” and “LOVE AGAIN,” the six-track continues her signature R&B sound while telling a narrative that explores grief, acceptance, and opening oneself up to learning how to love again. The project captures the cycle of love, loss, and everything in between, offering a relatable experience to many of her fans and listeners while acknowledging that love and healing aren’t always linear. Known as the Patron of Situationships, as voted by her fans, the singer-songwriter enters a new chapter in her music career, marked by the release of this project.

stories.title: Everton in talks to sign Jonathan Rowe from Bologna - football-talk.co.uk
stories.summary: Everton have made enquiries over a deal to sign exciting England U21 international winger Jonathan Rowe from Bologna this summer, according to TEAMtalk . Rowe progressed through every level of Norwich City’s academy. His displays for the development side earned him a senior debut in a 3-0 Premier League defeat to Crystal Palace on 28 December 2021. Although he’d go on to make 13 Premier League appearances, it was in the Championship where Rowe truly announced himself as one of English football’s brightest young prospects.

stories.title: Vyner braced for eye-opening Wrexham popularity in US
stories.summary: Wrexham defender Zak Vyner is looking forward to see for himself how popular the Welsh club are in the United States. Wrexham defender Zak Vyner is looking forward to see how popular the Welsh club are in the United States for himself. The Championship club, who recently faced Wisla Krakow in Poland and Manchester United in Finland, have travelled to the US for three games. Wrexham's previous two tours of America have attracted huge crowds due to their global profile under owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac and the success of the Welcome to Wrexham documentary series. "It's going to be a lot of hard work, but it'll be a great experience for me to get a taste of it," Vyner said.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron, typically found in Africa, Asia, and the Americas, has been spotted for the first time in the UK at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate in Somerset. Conservationists say the sighting follows efforts to restore freshwater habitats, which now support water voles, otters, and even white-tailed eagles. Experts hope the project will help reverse the UK’s loss of over 97% of its wetlands.

stories.title: Death of husband in HSE care leaves unanswered questions
stories.summary: The wife of a man who took his own life while receiving psychiatric treatment in Cork has questioned how he was able to do so while he was being held in a secure mental health unit. 67-year-old Mark Reilly was admitted to Cork University Hospital's Acute Mental Health Unit in March 2022 because he was suicidal. Cork University Hospital and the HSE have apologised for failings in Mark's care. Nancy Reilly said Mark, a former software engineer, community volunteer and teacher, had shown no signs of mental illness during their 37-year marriage before developing what she believes was a psychosis from late 2021.

stories.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced-labor justification
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s new global tariffs, imposed Thursday under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, target 60 economies with duties of 10% or 12.5% based on forced-labor enforcement, covering 99.4% of U.S. imports. Trading partners from Australia to Brazil have rejected the forced-labor justification but signaled continued negotiations rather than immediate retaliation. The measure replaces a temporary 10% tariff set to expire Friday, providing a more durable legal basis after the Supreme Court struck down earlier emergency tariffs in February.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

stories.title: Who am I? Guess Premier League star No 4
stories.summary: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible? Welcome to our Who am I? game. The rules are simple. Each day there's a new footballer and the challenge is to guess who they are in as few attempts as possible. After each wrong guess you unlock a new clue. Guess the answer after as few clues as possible to score more points. Three is a good score, four or five points is exceptional. So take part and return for more tomorrow. Today's player and clues have been set by BBC Sport's Flora Snelson. After more quizzes? Go to our dedicated Football Quizzes and Sports Quizzes pages and sign up for notifications to get the latest quizzes sent straight to your device.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

stories.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
stories.summary: MPs’ staff are demanding urgent security upgrades after a survey found 78% received threats and nearly one-third fear for their safety. Prime Minister Andy Burnham will announce a security review following the murder of Reform UK’s Ann Widdecombe in South Yorkshire this month. Joshua Kerry, 28, has been charged with her killing.

stories.title: My generation shouldn’t be scared of a care tax – we’re paying one already | Gaby Hinsliff
stories.summary: Generation X now faces a mounting care crisis as aging parents and young children demand equal attention, with many juggling full-time jobs and long commutes. A new prime minister’s pledge to reform social care could ease the financial and emotional strain on families already stretched thin. Without action, the burden will only grow as this generation shoulders the cost alone.

stories.title: 'Evidence lacking' for mental health monitoring tech
stories.summary: A UK inquiry has found no strong evidence that Oxevision, a camera and sensor system used in mental health wards, improves patient safety. The Lampard Inquiry, reviewing over 2,000 deaths in Essex mental health services since 2000, heard experts say existing studies on the technology are insufficient. The system’s maker claims it helped staff respond to 1,453 safety incidents in one trust over four years.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, credits a female veterans support group with improving her mental health after years of PTSD from multiple war-zone deployments. The group, expanding as more women join the military, now connects veterans nationwide, with the Ministry of Defence pledging increased NHS support. More dedicated services are expected as demand grows.

stories.title: Premium athletic brand to offer Klarna payments at Bicester Village store
stories.summary: A premium athletic apparel brand has partnered with a buy now pay later company across its UK stores. Lululemon, best known for yoga-inspired leggings and gym wear, has launched Klarna payments in its 21 stores. One of its stores is based at the luxury designer outlet Bicester Village, which opened in 2017. Bicester Village (Image: Oxford Mail) Customers can now use the flexible payment service in-store by scanning a QR code at checkout and completing the transaction through the Klarna app.

stories.title: Watch an orca ram a sunfish so hard that the fish explodes
stories.summary: How hard do you have to ram a fish to make it explode? Ask an orca. Barreling into large, heavy fish with enough force to blast them into instant chum is a previously unknown collaborative feeding strategy among orcas, scientifically called Orcinus orca, also known as killer whales. But in 2024 and 2025, divers captured underwater video of two such events in the Mexican Pacific Ocean and in the Gulf of California. The footage suggests that the brutal body slamming may be in service of a meal — or perhaps a form of play for the orcas, researchers reported Thursday in the journal Frontiers in Ethology.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
stories.summary: The tariffs target 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners, accounting for the vast majority of its imports, over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan and India. They came into effect on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expired. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: A Million-Panel Project
stories.summary: Utah’s new Green River Energy Center, featuring nearly one million solar panels and 500 batteries, went online in June 2026. The 400-megawatt solar and 400-megawatt battery facility will power Salt Lake City and surrounding areas. The project marks a shift from the region’s historic coal mining toward renewable energy.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.

stories.title: Burnham urged to 'build bridges' with sacked Starmerites
stories.summary: Andy Burnham needs to "build bridges" with the Starmerite minister he sacked during his reshuffle, Baroness Harriet Harman has told the Electoral Dysfunction podcast.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

offbeat.title: Italy in crisis: federation sounds out Pep Guardiola and Carlo Ancelotti for coach

Former Italy and AC Milan defender Paolo Maldini was named as the federation's new technical director at the start of the month and was present at a meeting with the Serie A clubs on Thursday, along with new FIGC president Giovanni Malagò and special advisor Leonardo. “Honestly, today we can't give news about what's happening.

Barreling into large, heavy fish with enough force to blast them into instant chum is a previously unknown collaborative feeding strategy among orcas, scientifically called Orcinus orca, also known as killer whales. But in 2024 and 2025, divers captured underwater video of two such events in the Mexican Pacific Ocean and in the Gulf of California. The footage suggests that the brutal body slamming may be in service of a meal — or perhaps a form of play for the orcas, researchers reported Thursday in the journal Frontiers in Ethology.

offbeat.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
offbeat.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear. Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now? Figure caption, Watch: A baby trafficking ring has been convicted - but what now for the babies? By Tessa Wong Reporting from Singapore and Astudestra Ajengrastri Reporting from Jakarta On Tuesday an Indonesian court convicted 19 people for baby trafficking, wrapping up a high-profile case - but leaving a key question unanswered. The members of the trafficking syndicate were found guilty of procuring at least 34 babies and selling them for adoption.

sports.title: Vyner braced for eye-opening Wrexham popularity in US
sports.summary: Wrexham defender Zak Vyner is looking forward to see for himself how popular the Welsh club are in the United States. Wrexham defender Zak Vyner is looking forward to see how popular the Welsh club are in the United States for himself. The Championship club, who recently faced Wisla Krakow in Poland and Manchester United in Finland, have travelled to the US for three games. Wrexham's previous two tours of America have attracted huge crowds due to their global profile under owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac and the success of the Welcome to Wrexham documentary series. "It's going to be a lot of hard work, but it'll be a great experience for me to get a taste of it," Vyner said.

sports.title: Who am I? Guess Premier League star No 4
sports.summary: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible? Welcome to our Who am I? game. The rules are simple. Each day there's a new footballer and the challenge is to guess who they are in as few attempts as possible. After each wrong guess you unlock a new clue. Guess the answer after as few clues as possible to score more points. Three is a good score, four or five points is exceptional. So take part and return for more tomorrow. Today's player and clues have been set by BBC Sport's Flora Snelson. After more quizzes? Go to our dedicated Football Quizzes and Sports Quizzes pages and sign up for notifications to get the latest quizzes sent straight to your device.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: A rare black-crowned night heron, typically found in Africa, Asia, and the Americas, has been spotted for the first time in the UK at the National Trust’s Holnicote Estate in Somerset. Conservationists say the sighting follows efforts to restore freshwater habitats, which now support water voles, otters, and even white-tailed eagles. Experts hope the project will help reverse the UK’s loss of over 97% of its wetlands.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

facts.title: A Million-Panel Project
facts.summary: Utah’s new Green River Energy Center, featuring nearly one million solar panels and 500 batteries, went online in June 2026. The 400-megawatt solar and 400-megawatt battery facility will power Salt Lake City and surrounding areas. The project marks a shift from the region’s historic coal mining toward renewable energy.

crisis.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced-labor justification
crisis.summary: President Donald Trump’s new global tariffs, imposed Thursday under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, target 60 economies with duties of 10% or 12.5% based on forced-labor enforcement, covering 99.4% of U.S. imports. Trading partners from Australia to Brazil have rejected the forced-labor justification but signaled continued negotiations rather than immediate retaliation. The measure replaces a temporary 10% tariff set to expire Friday, providing a more durable legal basis after the Supreme Court struck down earlier emergency tariffs in February.

crisis.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

crisis.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
crisis.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

health.title: 'Evidence lacking' for mental health monitoring tech
health.summary: A UK inquiry has found no strong evidence that Oxevision, a camera and sensor system used in mental health wards, improves patient safety. The Lampard Inquiry, reviewing over 2,000 deaths in Essex mental health services since 2000, heard experts say existing studies on the technology are insufficient. The system’s maker claims it helped staff respond to 1,453 safety incidents in one trust over four years.

health.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
health.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, credits a female veterans support group with improving her mental health after years of PTSD from multiple war-zone deployments. The group, expanding as more women join the military, now connects veterans nationwide, with the Ministry of Defence pledging increased NHS support. More dedicated services are expected as demand grows.

politics.title: Urgent need for more security after physical attacks and abuse, say MPs’ staff
politics.summary: MPs’ staff are demanding urgent security upgrades after a survey found 78% received threats and nearly one-third fear for their safety. Prime Minister Andy Burnham will announce a security review following the murder of Reform UK’s Ann Widdecombe in South Yorkshire this month. Joshua Kerry, 28, has been charged with her killing.

politics.title: My generation shouldn’t be scared of a care tax – we’re paying one already | Gaby Hinsliff
politics.summary: Generation X now faces a mounting care crisis as aging parents and young children demand equal attention, with many juggling full-time jobs and long commutes. A new prime minister’s pledge to reform social care could ease the financial and emotional strain on families already stretched thin. Without action, the burden will only grow as this generation shoulders the cost alone.

politics.title: Burnham urged to 'build bridges' with sacked Starmerites
politics.summary: Andy Burnham needs to "build bridges" with the Starmerite minister he sacked during his reshuffle, Baroness Harriet Harman has told the Electoral Dysfunction podcast.

breaking.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
breaking.summary: The tariffs target 60 trading partners over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners, accounting for the vast majority of its imports, over claims they failed to properly stop forced labour. The duties, ranging from 10% to 12. 5%, target key economic partners – including the UK, China, the European Union, Canada, Japan and India. They came into effect on Friday, as a temporary 10% tax on foreign goods introduced earlier this year expired. The move is the latest escalation in the global trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year.

breaking.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
breaking.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

tech.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
tech.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

entertainment.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
entertainment.summary: Sia’s greatest songs, like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" and "The Greatest," are anthems of strength, power, fortitude, invincibility, and watching the movie it’s fascinating to behold how her facelessness feeds that quality. She’s an abstract figure, a pair of lips singing, and this creates the sensation that the power she’s singing about doesn’t emanate from her. She’s channeling a force larger than herself — the rising specter of feminine triumph. She’s merely the vessel.
